revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Anagahayama Kofun]][[ja:穴ヶ葉山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Anagahayama Kofun | native_name =穴ヶ葉山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Anegahayama Kofun No.1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Anegahayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Anagahayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.56411111111111|N|131.16844444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kōge, Fukuoka|en|Kōge, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206598|lt=Kōge, Fukuoka|1=Kōge, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th - 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|33|47|N|131|10|08|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Anagahayama Kofun'''|穴ヶ葉山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Shimokarahara neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōge, Fukuoka|en|Kōge, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206598|lt=Kōge|1=Kōge, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Chikujō District, Fukuoka|en|Chikujō District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1072644|lt=Chikujō District|1=Chikujō District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1939.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139707|title=穴ヶ葉山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Anagahayama Kofun is located on the eastern slope of a hill on the left bank of the {{ill|Yamakuni River|en|Yamakuni River|qid=Q11467676|lt=Yamakuni River|1=Yamakuni River}}, which forms the border between {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita|1=Ōita Prefecture}} and Fukuoka prefectures. Designated Anagahana Kofun No.1, it is the main tumulus of a cluster of three ancient tombs. It is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style circular tumulus, with a diameter of 30 meters, surrounded by a six-meter wide horseshoe-shaped moat. It is orientated to the south-southwest. The ten-meter horizontal-passage [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is made of large monoliths. The walls of the passageway and burial chamber are decorated with line-carved murals, and themes include leaf patterns, fish, people, insects, and birds. The leaf pattern is especially large, measuring 52 cm in length, and the veins of the leaf are also expressed. Most [[decorated kofun]], especially in central and northern Kyushu, have painted patterns; whereas line-engraved decorations are more common in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori|1=Tottori Prefecture}} and {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}s. The tumulus is thought to have been built between the end of the 6th century and the early 7th century, based on artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1993 to 1995 and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The Anagahayama Kofun is listed in the family registry of Tozato, Kōge District, dated 702, which is preserved at the [[Shōsōin]] in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}. Per this document, it was the tomb of an influential ''{{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|qid=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin}}'' immigrant clan from mainland Asia who had settled in the area. Anagahana Kofun No.2 is a much smaller ''enpun''-style tumulus with a diameter of ten meters, and Anagahana Kofun No.3 is likewise an ''enpun''-style tumulus with a diameter of 19 meters. Anagahana Kofun No.3 also has some line decorations, but neither tumulus is part of the National Historic Site. The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Nakatsu Station (Ōita)|en|Nakatsu Station (Ōita)|qid=Q5360376|lt=Nakatsu Station|1=Nakatsu Station (Ōita)}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In former Ohira Village to the south was the Anagahayama Minami Kofun cluster with more than ten ''kofun''; however six were destroyed to make way for an industrial waste disposal site. During excavations in 1983, a line-engraved mural was discovered in the stone chamber of Anagahayama Minami Kofun No. 3, so only this tumulus was preserved within the grounds of the disposal site. It is an ''enpun''-style tumulus with a diameter of 13 meters, surrounded by a one-meter wide moat. The horizontal entry stone burial chamber has a total length of 5.6 meters. Patterns that appear to be leaves can be seen in three places. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Anagahayama Kofun Group|qq=}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=92 Cultural Properties of Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14823 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} Anagahayama Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
